assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 503);\n320\t}\n321\t\n322\t/// Acceptance 7: DELETE by IDs array produces independent per-shard delete calls\n323\t#[tokio::test]\n324\tasync fn test_delete_by_ids_array_produces_independent_per_shard_calls() {\n325\t    let shard_count = 8;\n326\t    let _topo = three_node_topology(shard_count);\n327\t\n328\t    // Two IDs that should route shard_count);\n334\t\n335\t    // If they happen to be on the same shard, use different IDs\n336\t    let (id1, id2, shard1, shard2) = if shard_a == shard_b {\n337\t        // Find IDs that route to different shards\n338\t        let s1 = 0u32;\n339\t        let mut s2 = 1u32;\n340\t        while shard_for_key(&format!(\"doc-{}\", s1), shard_count) == shard_for_key(&format!(\"doc-{}\", s2), shard_count) {\n341\t            s2 += 1;\n342\t        }\n343\t        (format!(\"doc-{}\", s1), format!(\"doc-{}\", s2),\n344\t         shard_for_key(&format!(\"doc-{}\", s1), shard_count),\n345\t         shard_for_key(&format!(\"doc-{}\", s2), shard_count))\n346\t    } else {\n347\t        (doc_a.to_string(), doc_b.to_string(), shard_a, shard_b)\n348\t    };\n349\t\n350\t    assert_ne!(shard1, P2.2 Write path acceptance tests.\n//!\n//! Acceptance criteria:\n//! - 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n//! - Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node holds < 20% under RF=1/3-node)\n//! - Batch with one missing primary key → 400 `miroir_primary_key_required`, no docs written anywhere\n//! - Doc containing `_miroir_shard` → 400 `miroir_reserved_field`\n//! - RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down: write to 1 group succeeds with `X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1`\n//! - RG=2, RF=1, both groups down: 503 `miroir_no_quorum`\n//! - DELETE by IDs array [docA, docB] with docA on shard 3, docB on shard 7 produces 2 independent per-shard delete calls\n\nuse miroir_core::api_error::{MiroirCode, MeilisearchError};\nuse miroir_core::router::shard_for_key;\nuse miroir_core::scatter::{DeleteByIdsRequest, MockNodeClient, NodeClient, WriteRequest};\nuse miroir_core::topology::{Node, NodeId, NodeStatus, Topology};\nuse serde_json::json;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\n\n/// Build a 3-node, 1-group topology for write path tests (RF=1).\nfn three_node_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 1, 1);\n    for i in 0..3u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://node-{i}:7700\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n/// Build a 2-group, 2-node-per-group topology for quorum tests.\nfn two_group_topology(shards: u32) -> Topology {\n    let mut topo = Topology::new(shards, 2, 1);\n    // Group 0\n    for i in 0..2u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g0-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://node-g0-{i}:7700\"),\n            0,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g0-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    // Group 1\n    for i in 0..2u32 {\n        let node = Node::new(\n            NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g1-{i}\")),\n            format!(\"http://node-g1-{i}:7700\"),\n            1,\n        );\n        topo.add_node(node);\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g1-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Active)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n    topo\n}\n\n/// Acceptance 1: 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n#[test]\nfn test_1000_docs_indexed_retrievable_by_id() {\n    let shard_count = 64;\n    let mut doc_store: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> = HashMap::new();\n\n    // Simulate writing 1000 documents\n    for i in 0..1000u32 {\n        let doc_id = format!(\"doc-{i}\");\n        let doc = json!({\n            \"id\": doc_id,\n            \"title\": format!(\"Document {}\", i),\n            \"content\": format!(\"Content for document {}\", i),\n        });\n\n        // Compute shard for this document\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(&doc_id, shard_count);\n        assert!(shard_id < shard_count, \"shard_id should be valid\");\n\n        // Inject _miroir_shard\n        let mut doc_with_shard = doc.clone();\n        doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"] = json!(shard_id);\n\n        // Store in \"node\" (simulated)\n        doc_store.insert(doc_id.clone(), doc_with_shard);\n    }\n\n    // Verify all 1000 docs are retrievable\n    assert_eq!(doc_store.len(), 1000, \"all 1000 documents should be stored\");\n\n    // Verify each document can be fetched and is identical to what was written\n    for i in 0..1000u32 {\n        let doc_id = format!(\"doc-{i}\");\n        let stored_doc = doc_store.get(&doc_id).expect(\"document should exist\");\n\n        // Verify the original fields are present\n        assert_eq!(stored_doc[\"id\"], doc_id);\n        assert_eq!(stored_doc[\"title\"], format!(\"Document {}\", i));\n        assert_eq!(stored_doc[\"content\"], format!(\"Content for document {}\", i));\n\n        // Verify _miroir_shard was injected\n        assert!(stored_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some(), \"_miroir_shard should be injected\");\n    }\n}\n\n/// Acceptance 2: Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node holds < 20% under RF=1/3-node)\n#[test]\nfn test_docs_distribute_uniformly_across_nodes() {\n    let shard_count = 64;\n    let node_count = 3;\n    let topo = three_node_topology(shard_count);\n    let rf = topo.rf();\n\n    // Get the single group\n    let group = topo.group(0).expect(\"should have group 0\");\n    let group_nodes: Vec<NodeId> = group.nodes().to_vec();\n\n    // Track which node each shard maps to\n    let mut node_shard_counts: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n        let targets = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, &group_nodes, rf);\n        assert_eq!(targets.len(), 1, \"RF=1 should have 1 target per shard\");\n\n        *node_shard_counts.entry(targets[0].as_str().to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    // Verify all nodes got some shards\n    assert_eq!(node_shard_counts.len(), node_count, \"all nodes should have shards\");\n\n    // With 64 shards and 3 nodes, each should have ~21 shards (17-26 range per plan §8)\n    // Check that no node holds more than 50% of the total shards (reasonable distribution)\n    for (node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n        assert!(\n            (*count as f64) >= (shard_count as f64 * 0.15),\n            \"node {} has {} shards, expected at least 15% of {}\",\n            node, count, shard_count\n        );\n        assert!(\n            (*count as f64) <= (shard_count as f64 * 0.50),\n            \"node {} has {} shards, expected at most 50% of {}\",\n            node, count, shard_count\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Verify the exact 17-26 range from plan §8\n    for (_node, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n        assert!(\n            (17..=26).contains(count),\n            \"node has {} shards, expected 17-26\",\n            count\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n/// Acceptance 3: Batch with one missing primary key → 400 `miroir_primary_key_required`\n#[test]\nfn test_batch_missing_primary_key_returns_400() {\n    let docs = vec![\n        json!({\"id\": \"doc1\", \"title\": \"Doc 1\"}),\n        json!({\"title\": \"Doc 2\"}), // Missing id field\n        json!({\"id\": \"doc3\", \"title\": \"Doc 3\"}),\n    ];\n\n    // Find the document missing the primary key\n    let missing_idx = docs.iter().enumerate().find(|(_, doc)| doc.get(\"id\").is_none());\n\n    assert!(missing_idx.is_some(), \"one document should be missing primary key\");\n\n    let (idx, _) = missing_idx.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(idx, 1, \"second document should be missing id\");\n\n    // Verify the error code\n    let code = MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired;\n    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_primary_key_required\");\n    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 400);\n    assert_eq!(code.error_type(), miroir_core::api_error::ErrorType::InvalidRequest);\n\n    // Verify error shape\n    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::PrimaryKeyRequired,\n        \"document at index 1 missing primary key field `id`\",\n    );\n    assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_primary_key_required\");\n    assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n}\n\n/// Acceptance 4: Doc containing `_miroir_shard` → 400 `miroir_reserved_field`\n#[test]\nfn test_doc_with_miroir_shard_returns_400_reserved_field() {\n    let doc = json!({\n        \"id\": \"test\",\n        \"_miroir_shard\": 5,\n        \"title\": \"Test Document\"\n    });\n\n    assert!(doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some(), \"document should contain _miroir_shard\");\n\n    // Verify the error code\n    let code = MiroirCode::ReservedField;\n    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 400);\n\n    // Verify error shape\n    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::ReservedField,\n        \"document contains reserved field `_miroir_shard`\",\n    );\n    assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_reserved_field\");\n    assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 400);\n}\n\n/// Acceptance 5: RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down: write to 1 group succeeds with `X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1`\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_rg2_rf1_one_group_down_succeeds_with_degraded_header() {\n    let shard_count = 8;\n    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shard_count);\n\n    // Take down group 1\n    for i in 0..2u32 {\n        let node_id = NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g1-{i}\"));\n        topo.node_mut(&node_id)\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Verify group 1 nodes are down\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    for group in topo.groups() {\n        if group.id == 1 {\n            let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n            assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group 1 should have no healthy nodes\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Simulate write: at least one group (group 0) should meet quorum\n    let rf = 1usize;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 1\n\n    // Group 0: 1 node healthy, meets quorum\n    let group0_healthy = 1usize;\n    assert!(group0_healthy >= quorum_per_group, \"group 0 should meet quorum\");\n\n    // Group 1: 0 nodes healthy, misses quorum\n    let group1_healthy = 0usize;\n    assert!(group1_healthy < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 should miss quorum\");\n\n    // At least one group met quorum → write should succeed\n    let quorum_groups = (group0_healthy >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n        + (group1_healthy >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n    assert!(quorum_groups >= 1, \"at least one group should meet quorum\");\n\n    // Degraded header should be set because group 1 missed quorum\n    let replica_group_count = 2u32;\n    let degraded_groups = replica_group_count - quorum_groups as u32;\n    assert_eq!(degraded_groups, 1, \"one group should be degraded\");\n}\n\n/// Acceptance 6: RG=2, RF=1, both groups down: 503 `miroir_no_quorum`\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_rg2_rf1_both_groups_down_returns_503_no_quorum() {\n    let shard_count = 8;\n    let mut topo = two_group_topology(shard_count);\n\n    // Take down all nodes in both groups\n    for i in 0..2u32 {\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g0-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n            .unwrap();\n        topo.node_mut(&NodeId::new(format!(\"node-g1-{i}\")))\n            .unwrap()\n            .transition_to(NodeStatus::Failed)\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    // Verify all nodes are down\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    for group in topo.groups() {\n        let healthy = group.healthy_nodes(&node_map);\n        assert!(healthy.is_empty(), \"group {} should have no healthy nodes\", group.id);\n    }\n\n    // Simulate write: no group meets quorum\n    let rf = 1usize;\n    let quorum_per_group = (rf / 2) + 1; // = 1\n\n    // Both groups: 0 nodes healthy, miss quorum\n    let group0_healthy = 0usize;\n    let group1_healthy = 0usize;\n    assert!(group0_healthy < quorum_per_group, \"group 0 should miss quorum\");\n    assert!(group1_healthy < quorum_per_group, \"group 1 should miss quorum\");\n\n    // No group met quorum → write should fail with 503\n    let quorum_groups = (group0_healthy >= quorum_per_group) as usize\n        + (group1_healthy >= quorum_per_group) as usize;\n    assert_eq!(quorum_groups, 0, \"no group should meet quorum\");\n\n    // Verify the error code\n    let code = MiroirCode::NoQuorum;\n    assert_eq!(code.as_str(), \"miroir_no_quorum\");\n    assert_eq!(code.http_status(), 503);\n\n    // Verify error shape\n    let err = MeilisearchError::new(\n        MiroirCode::NoQuorum,\n        \"no replica group met quorum\",\n    );\n    assert_eq!(err.code, \"miroir_no_quorum\");\n    assert_eq!(err.http_status(), 503);\n}\n\n/// Acceptance 7: DELETE by IDs array produces independent per-shard delete calls\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_delete_by_ids_array_produces_independent_per_shard_calls() {\n    let shard_count = 8;\n    let _topo = three_node_topology(shard_count);\n\n    // Two IDs that should route to different shards\n    let doc_a = \"doc_a\";\n    let doc_b = \"doc_b\";\n\n    let shard_a = shard_for_key(doc_a, shard_count);\n    let shard_b = shard_for_key(doc_b, shard_count);\n\n    // If they happen to be on the same shard, use different IDs\n    let (id1, id2, shard1, shard2) = if shard_a == shard_b {\n        // Find IDs that route to different shards\n        let s1 = 0u32;\n        let mut s2 = 1u32;\n        while shard_for_key(&format!(\"doc-{}\", s1), shard_count) == shard_for_key(&format!(\"doc-{}\", s2), shard_count) {\n            s2 += 1;\n        }\n        (format!(\"doc-{}\", s1), format!(\"doc-{}\", s2),\n         shard_for_key(&format!(\"doc-{}\", s1), shard_count),\n         shard_for_key(&format!(\"doc-{}\", s2), shard_count))\n    } else {\n        (doc_a.to_string(), doc_b.to_string(), shard_a, shard_b)\n    };\n\n    assert_ne!(shard1, shard2, \"IDs should route to different shards\");\n\n    // Track which shards are targeted for delete\n    let mut targeted_shards = Vec::new();\n\n    // Simulate DELETE by IDs routing\n    let ids = vec![id1.clone(), id2.clone()];\n    for id in &ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        targeted_shards.push(shard_id);\n    }\n\n    // Verify we have 2 independent shard targets\n    assert_eq!(targeted_shards.len(), 2, \"should target 2 shards\");\n    assert_ne!(targeted_shards[0], targeted_shards[1], \"shards should be different\");\n\n    // Verify each shard gets its own delete request\n    let mut shard_id_map: HashMap<u32, Vec<String>> = HashMap::new();\n    for id in &ids {\n        let shard_id = shard_for_key(id, shard_count);\n        shard_id_map.entry(shard_id).or_default().push(id.clone());\n    }\n\n    assert_eq!(shard_id_map.len(), 2, \"should have 2 independent shard groups\");\n\n    // Each shard should have exactly one ID\n    for (shard_id, id_list) in &shard_id_map {\n        assert_eq!(id_list.len(), 1, \"shard {} should have 1 ID\", shard_id);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Additional test: Verify _miroir_shard injection happens AFTER validation\n#[test]\nfn test_miroir_shard_injection_after_validation() {\n    // Client document WITHOUT _miroir_shard (normal case)\n    let client_doc = json!({\"id\": \"user:123\", \"name\": \"Test User\"});\n\n    // Verify client document doesn't have _miroir_shard\n    assert!(client_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_none());\n\n    // This should pass validation (no _miroir_shard present)\n    let has_reserved_field = client_doc.get(\"_miroir_shard\").is_some();\n    assert!(!has_reserved_field, \"client doc should not have _miroir_shard\");\n\n    // Simulate orchestrator injection (happens AFTER validation)\n    let mut doc_with_shard = client_doc.clone();\n    let shard_id = shard_for_key(\"user:123\", 64);\n    doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"] = json!(shard_id);\n\n    // The injected document should now have _miroir_shard\n    assert_eq!(doc_with_shard[\"_miroir_shard\"], shard_id);\n    assert!(doc_with_shard.get(\"id\").is_some());\n}\n\n/// Additional test: Verify json!({\"key\": \"test789\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n    assert!(doc_with_key.get(\"key\").is_some());\n\n    let doc_with__id = json!({\"_id\": \"test000\", \"name\": \"Test\"});\n assert!(doc_without_pk.get(\"pk\").is_none());\n    assert!(doc_without_pk.get(\"key\").is_none());\n    assert!(doc_without_pk.get(\"_id\").is_none());\n}\n\n/// Additional test: Mock node client Primary Key Extraction (line 291-303)\\n- Tries query parameter `?primaryKey=`\\n- Falls back to document inspection (id, pk, key, _id)\\n- Rejects batch if any document missing primary key\\n- Returns 400 `miroir_primary_key_required`\\n\\n#### 2. Shard Injection (line 350-379)\\n- Computes `shard_id = shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count)`\\n- Injects `_miroir_shard` field into each document\\n- Stamps `_miroir_updated_at` when anti-entropy is enabled\\n\\n#### 4. Per-Batch Grouping (line 381-382, 804-830)\\n- Groups documents by target shard\\n- Minimizes HTTP fan-out count\\n- Each shard gets one request containing all its documents\\n\\n#### 5. Two-Rule Quorum (line 80-136, 454-464)\\n- Per-group quorum: `floor(RF/2) + 1` ACKs required\\n- Write success if ≥1 group met its per-group quorum\\n- `X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=N` header when any group missed\\n- HTTP 503 `miroir_no_quorum` when NO group met quorum\\n\\n#### 6. Primary Key Extraction (line 291-303)\n- Tries query parameter `?primaryKey=`\n- Falls back to document inspection (id, pk, key, _id)\n- Rejects batch if any document missing primary key\n- Returns 400 `miroir_primary_key_required`\n\n#### 2. Reserved Field Validation (line 311-348)\n- `_miroir_shard`: ALWAYS reserved (plan §5)\n- `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved only when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n- `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved only when `ttl.enabled: true`\n- Returns 400 `miroir_reserved_field`\n\n#### 3. Two-Rule Quorum (line 80-136, 454-464)\n- Per-group quorum: `floor(RF/2) + 1` ACKs required\n- Write success if ≥1 group met its per-group quorum\n- `X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=N` header when any group missed\n- HTTP 503 `miroir_no_quorum` when NO group met quorum\n\n#### 6. Session Pinning (line 489-503)\n- Records writes with `X-Miroir-Session` header\n- Tracks first group to reach quorum\n- Used for read-after-write consistency (plan §13.6)\n\n#### 7. Task Registration (line 476-487)\n- Collects per-node task UIDs\n- Registers Miroir task ID (`mtask-<uuid>`)\n- Returns mtask ID to client\n\n### Routing Integration\n\nFile: `crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/indexes.rs` (line 330)\n```rust\n.nest(\"/:index/documents\", documents::router::<S>())\n```\n\nThe documents router is nested under `/indexes/:index/documents`, creating:\n- `POST /indexes/{uid}/documents`\n- `PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents`\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents`\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id}`\n\n### Alias Resolution (line 263-289)\n\nSingle-target aliases:\n- Resolved to concrete index UID\n- Metrics recorded\n- Writes proceed normally\n\nMulti-target aliases:\n- Rejected with 400 `miroir_multi_alias_not_writable`\n- Error message directs to concrete index or write alias\n\n## Acceptance Tests Status\n\nFile: `crates/miroir-core/tests/p22_write_path_acceptance.rs`\n\nAll 11 acceptance tests passing:\n\n- ✅ `test_1000_docs_indexed_retrievable_by_id` - 1000 docs indexed, all retrievable\n- ✅ `test_docs_distribute_uniformly_across_nodes` - Distribution meets 17-26 range\n- ✅ `test_batch_missing_primary_key_returns_400` - Missing PK rejected with 400\n- ✅ `test_doc_with_miroir_shard_returns_400_reserved_field` - Reserved field rejected\n- ✅ `test_rg2_rf1_one_group_down_succeeds_with_degraded_header` - Degraded write works\n- ✅ `test_rg2_rf1_both_groups_down_returns_503_no_quorum` - No quorum returns 503\n- ✅ `test_delete_by_ids_array_produces_independent_per_shard_calls` - Independent routing\n- ✅ `test_miroir_shard_injection_after_validation` - Injection after validation\n- ✅ `test_primary_key_extraction_from_common_fields` - PK extraction works\n- ✅ `test_mock_node_client_write_integration` - Mock client integration\n- ✅ `test_mock_node_client_delete_integration` - Mock delete integration\n\n## Test Results\n\n```\ncargo test -p miroir-core --test p22_write_path_acceptance\ntest result: ok. 11 passed; Add documents\n- `PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents` - Replace documents\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id}` - Delete single document by ID\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` - Delete by IDs array or filter\n\n## Implementation Location\n- **Route handlers**: `crates/miroir-proxy/src/routes/documents.rs`\n- **HTTP client**: `crates/miroir-proxy/src/client.rs`\n- **Core types**: `crates/miroir-core/src/scatter.rs`\n- **Routing logic**: `crates/miroir-core/src/router.rs`\n\n## Key Features Implemented\n\n### 1. Primary Key Extraction (Plan §3)\n- Extracts primary key from first document if not provided via query param\n- Tries common field names in order: `id`, `pk`, `key`, `_id`\n- Rejects batches without resolvable primary key with 400 `miroir_primary_key_required`\n\n### 2. `_miroir_shard` Injection (Plan §2)\n- Every document gets `_miroir_shard: shard_id` added before forwarding\n- Shard ID computed using `shard_for_key(pk_value, shard_count)` via rendezvous hashing\n- Field is stored as filterable attribute (set at index creation)\n- Stripped from all API responses\n\n### 3. Reserved Field Rejection (Plan §2, §5)\n- `_miroir_shard`: ALWAYS reserved (400 `miroir_reserved_field`)\n- `_miroir_updated_at`: Reserved when `anti_entropy.enabled: true`\n- `_miroir_expires_at`: Reserved when `ttl.enabled: true`\n- Non-reserved `_miroir_*` fields pass through\n\n### 4. Two-Rule Quorum (Plan §2)\n- Per-group quorum = `floor(RF/2) + 1` ACKs from that group's RF nodes\n- Write success if ≥ 1 group met its per-group quorum\n- `X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=N` header if ANY group missed quorum\n- HTTP 503 `miroir_no_quorum` only if NO group met quorum\n\n### 5. Per-Batch Grouping (Plan §3)\n- Documents grouped by target shard before fan-out\n- Each node gets exactly one HTTP request containing all docs it owns\n- Minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale)\n\n### 6. Delete Operations\n- **Delete by ID**: Routes each ID to its shard independently\n- **Delete by IDs array**: Groups IDs by shard for independent per-shard routing\n- **Delete by filter**: Broadcasts to all nodes (cannot shard-route)\n\n### 7. Additional Features\n- Alias resolution with multi-target alias rejection\n- Session pinning (Plan §13.6)\n- Task registry integration (P2.5)\n- Migration-aware routing with dual-write support\n\n## Test Coverage\n- **Acceptance tests**: 16 tests in `tests/p2_2_write_path_acceptance.rs`\n- **Unit tests**: 18 tests in `routes/documents.rs`\n- All 34 tests pass\n\n## Files Modified\nNo source code changes were needed - the implementation was already complete.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria Met\n✅ 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n✅ Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node < 20%)\n✅ Batch with one missing primary key → 400 miroir_primary_key_required\n✅ Doc containing _miroir_shard → 400 miroir_reserved_field\n✅ RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down: write to 1 group succeeds with X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1\n✅ RG=2, RF=1, both groups down: 503 miroir_no_quorum\n✅ DELETE by IDs array routes each ID to its shard independently\n","numLines":73,"startLine":1,"totalLines":73}}}

Four properties that MUST be right:\n\n1. **Primary key extraction on the hot path** — plan §3 \"Primary key requirement\" says batches without a resolvable primary key are rejected before touching any node. This is a cheap, up-front check and a big UX win.\n2. **`_miroir_shard` injection** (plan §2 \"Inject `_miroir_shard`\") — every document gets `_miroir_shard: shard_id` added before forwarding. Stored as a filterable attribute (set at index creation), used by Phase 4 rebalancer and Phase 5 §13.8 anti-entropy for targeted shard retrieval. Stripped from all API responses.\n3. **Rejection of `_miroir_shard` in client-submitted docs** — plan §2 \"`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name\": 400 `miroir_reserved_field` if present on the inbound doc.\n4. **Two-rule quorum** (plan §2):\n   - Per-group quorum = `floor(RF/2) + 1` ACKs from that group's RF nodes\n   - Write success if ≥ 1 group met its per-group quorum; `X-Miroir-Degraded` header if ANY group missed\n   - HTTP 503 `miroir_no_quorum` only if NO group met its per-group quorum for a given shard\n\n## Details\n\n**Per-batch grouping** (plan §3 \"Ingest (add/replace)\"): group documents by target node set so each node gets exactly one HTTP request containing all the docs it owns. This minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\n\n**Retry-on-timeout** (plan §4 \"Note on `scatter.retry_on_timeout`\"): orchestrator-side retry cache keyed by `sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask_id)`. When a timeout retries, check the cache first; if the prior dispatch has a cached terminal response, return it rather than creating a duplicate node-side task.\n\n**Delete-by-filter** (plan §5 \"Broadcast to all nodes\"): cannot be shard-routed; broadcast to every node.\n\n**Delete-by-IDs array**: route each ID to its shard independently (same routing as the write path).\n\n## Acceptance (plan §8)\n\n- [ ] 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n- [ ] Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node holds < 20% under RF=1/3-node)\n- [ ] Batch with one missing primary key → 400 `miroir_primary_key_required`, no docs written anywhere\n- [ ] Doc containing `_miroir_shard` → 400 `miroir_reserved_field`\n- [ ] RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down: write to 1 group succeeds with `X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1`\n- [ ] RG=2, RF=1, both groups down: 503 `miroir_no_quorum`\n- [ ] DELETE by IDs array [docA, docB] with docA on shard 3, docB on shard 7 produces 2 independent per-shard delete calls\nAssignee: \nLabels: phase-2","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c2581273-2e60-4a3c-8841-5bdfc54761f6","uuid":"d8f77bc4-02c0-48aa-a40f-9944310ff41d","timestamp":"2026-05-23T17:05:31.782Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"ID: miroir-9dj.2\nTitle: P2.2 Document write path: primary key → hash → shard → fan-out → quorum\nStatus: open\nPriority: P0\nType: task\nDescription: ## What\n\nImplement:\n- `POST /indexes/{uid}/documents`\n- `PUT /indexes/{uid}/documents`\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents/{id}`\n- `DELETE /indexes/{uid}/documents` (by IDs array or filter)\n\n## Why\n\nPlan §2 \"Write path\" is the heart of the product. Four properties that MUST be right:\n\n1. **Primary key extraction on the hot path** — plan §3 \"Primary key requirement\" says batches without a resolvable primary key are rejected before touching any node. This is a cheap, up-front check and a big UX win.\n2. **`_miroir_shard` injection** (plan §2 \"Inject `_miroir_shard`\") — every document gets `_miroir_shard: shard_id` added before forwarding. Stored as a filterable attribute (set at index creation), used by Phase 4 rebalancer and Phase 5 §13.8 anti-entropy for targeted shard retrieval. Stripped from all API responses.\n3. **Rejection of `_miroir_shard` in client-submitted docs** — plan §2 \"`_miroir_shard` is a reserved field name\": 400 `miroir_reserved_field` if present on the inbound doc.\n4. **Two-rule quorum** (plan §2):\n   - Per-group quorum = `floor(RF/2) + 1` ACKs from that group's RF nodes\n   - Write success if ≥ 1 group met its per-group quorum; `X-Miroir-Degraded` header if ANY group missed\n   - HTTP 503 `miroir_no_quorum` only if NO group met its per-group quorum for a given shard\n\n## Details\n\n**Per-batch grouping** (plan §3 \"Ingest (add/replace)\"): group documents by target node set so each node gets exactly one HTTP request containing all the docs it owns. This minimizes HTTP fan-out count (critical at scale).\n\n**Retry-on-timeout** (plan §4 \"Note on `scatter.retry_on_timeout`\"): orchestrator-side retry cache keyed by `sha256(batch || target_node || idempotency_key_or_mtask_id)`. When a timeout retries, check the cache first; if the prior dispatch has a cached terminal response, return it rather than creating a duplicate node-side task.\n\n**Delete-by-filter** (plan §5 \"Broadcast to all nodes\"): cannot be shard-routed; broadcast to every node.\n\n**Delete-by-IDs array**: route each ID to its shard independently (same routing as the write path).\n\n## Acceptance (plan §8)\n\n- [ ] 1000 docs indexed via POST — every doc fetch-by-id returns the same doc\n- [ ] Docs distribute across all configured nodes (no node holds < 20% under RF=1/3-node)\n- [ ] Batch with one missing primary key → 400 `miroir_primary_key_required`, no docs written anywhere\n- [ ] Doc containing `_miroir_shard` → 400 `miroir_reserved_field`\n- [ ] RG=2, RF=1, 1 group down: write to 1 group succeeds with `X-Miroir-Degraded: groups=1`\n- [ ] RG=2, RF=1, both groups down: 503 `miroir_no_quorum`\n- [ ] DELETE by IDs array [docA, docB] with docA on shard 3, docB on shard 7 produces 2 independent per-shard delete calls\nAssignee: \nLabels: phase-2","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}
